findingDetails.title: Validator connector claim is not enforced for ordinary box diagrams
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md promises connector-presence validation and an error for boxes without arrows/connectors, but the script counts `└─`, a standard box border token in the packet's own assets, as a connector marker.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either remove `└─` from connector detection and use connector-only tokens, or revise the docs to disclose the actual best-effort limitation and require manual connector review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed SKILL.md validator contract, notation reference, packet validator script, and owned simple workflow asset using `└─` as a box border.

noveltyJustification: First iteration in a fresh packet found one new weighted P1 and no prior accumulated findings.

ruledOut: SKILL.md is not a pointer-only stub.

focus: Reference dissection quality, duplication boundaries, README route-map, single-concern references, and assets/back-link fidelity

findingDetails.title: Top-level README still carries executable workflow steps instead of staying purely route-map oriented
findingDetails.evidence: README identifies SKILL.md as authoritative and maps packet contents, but its Quick Start gives a five-step execution workflow that duplicates part of SKILL.md's primary numbered creation workflow.
findingDetails.recommendation: Shrink README Quick Start to a route-map pointer to SKILL.md §6 and references/README.md, keeping only a minimal invocation example if needed.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared README route-map and quick-start sections with SKILL.md primary workflow; checked reference files separately and did not find the same duplicate numbered workflow pattern there.

noveltyJustification: Added one new P2 (weight 1) against accumulated active weight 6 (prior P1 weight 5 plus new P2 weight 1).

findingDetails.title: Preferred arrow notation drifts from the validator's actual connector detector
findingDetails.evidence: SKILL.md recommends `▼` and `──▶`, while validate_flowchart.sh only counts `→`, `↓`, `├─`, or `└─` as arrow/branch markers; notation_and_validator.md describes the script detector accurately, leaving the primary workflow and validator vocabulary misaligned.
findingDetails.recommendation: Either add `▼` and `▶`/`──▶` to check_arrows, or change SKILL.md preferred arrows to the glyphs the validator recognizes.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Compared SKILL.md notation rules, notation_and_validator.md connector mechanics, and validate_flowchart.sh grep detector; ran a preferred-vertical fixture and observed exit 0 due existing border-counting behavior, so this is drift risk rather than an additional blocking failure.

noveltyJustification: Added one new P2 (weight 1) against accumulated active weight 7 (prior P1 weight 5 plus two P2 weights).
